message:
fix(http,tools): flush TLS write buffer on zero-byte retry and harden bash temp file spill error handling

http/client.rs:
- Added an explicit poll_flush() call before sleeping when
  write_all_with_retry encounters a zero-byte write. Without the
  flush, TLS-layer buffering can cause the retry loop to make no
  progress — the underlying transport has data to send but the write
  returns 0 because the TLS record buffer is full, not because the
  socket is busy.

tools.rs (ingest_bash_chunk):
- Restructured temp file creation and async open into match-based
  error handling that gracefully degrades instead of returning hard
  errors. If the sync file creation fails, spill_failed is set and
  the chunk continues to be buffered in memory rather than aborting
  the entire bash output pipeline.
- TOCTOU inode validation now logs a warning and marks spill_failed
  instead of returning a tool error, preventing a race condition
  between temp file creation and async open from crashing the bash
  tool mid-execution.
- Async open failure is similarly handled via spill_failed rather
  than a hard error, improving resilience on systems where /tmp may
  be temporarily unavailable or permissions are restrictive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
